cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why Ms. Leibfried asked the author to recite a passage in front of the class. The options are A to D. First, I need to look back at the passage provided. The relevant part is when the author talks about the big project being a book report, which was usually read aloud. But Ms. Leibfried suggested that the author do something "a little different" and instead of a report, she recited a passage from "The Bell Jar." The author was initially very shy, the only nonwhite student, and felt out of place. Ms. Leibfried noticed her interest in reading and writing and made her feel less shy. The act of reciting was a way for the author to step out of her comfort zone. When she did recite, she felt nervous, but the class applauded, and Ms. Leibfried praised her for choosing a sensitive piece and delivering it beautifully. This made the author feel confident for the first time. So why did Ms. Leibfried suggest this? Option B says to make her earn the respect of her classmates. But the result was that the class applauded, but the motivation might be different. Option D is about building relationships with others. Option C is about
